title: Plan For Austin — RV-as-launchpad, ISAA cert, dad's legacy on-ramp
html:
Plan For Austin — RV-as-launchpad, ISAA cert, dad's legacy on-ramp

Source: 2025-08-13 Google Doc. The mid-summer pivot doc — written between the May scout plan and the Oct arrival. The RV option enters the chat. Multi-tab brain-dump of every variation Yahir was running.

TL;DR

  • The RV pivot: dad offered Yahir a 2016 RV. New plan = use RV as a "beachhead" / military-style launchpad outside Austin city ($500-800/month RV park rent vs $1,100 apartment), then upgrade to apartment once income stabilizes. Framed as "minimum work for maximum freedom."
  • The ISAA personal-training plan: cert costs ~$860, planned to fund it by enrolling in an OSU class as a placeholder, drawing the remaining $1,380 scholarship money, then dropping the class within the refund window. Goal: certified by Aug 8th. (This evolves into the NaviWell Health Coach role instead of standalone PT.)
  • The Cam-or-solo fork: if Cam doesn't commit, Yahir does the RV alone + maybe gets a motorcycle (Sterling). If Cam commits, they get a 2BR apartment. The doc treats both as live options — by Oct, Cam isn't coming.
  • Apartment shortlist (Tab 2): The Fielder, Artisan, City View at Mueller, Aspect, Forest Park, Terrace Cove. Filtered by "startup-life" criteria (workflow independence, focus layout).
  • The dad-legacy frame: "This is how my dad can leave his legacy, he can help me on ramp to greatness. I am indebted to him for this option" — first time Yahir frames the RV gift as a debt of gratitude from his father, contrast with the Sept 2024 dad DUI doc.
  • Brotein note: drops a bug report mid-doc — the Brotein website video isn't loading reliably, suggests static image fallback.

Pattern observed

This doc is planning under high uncertainty — Yahir is essentially modeling 5 different futures in parallel (RV solo, RV w/ Cam, apartment solo, apartment w/ Cam, delay-and-grind-Oregon). Each tab is a different scenario. The throughline that doesn't change: "I am ambitious, I want this, I can taste it, I can see it, and I have the courage to speak it." — same manifesto cadence that appears in the Aug 14 Emily note one day later and the Nov "we're gonna make it" doc.

Also notable: the mom providing income proof solution surfaces here ("Need proof of income for apartment (mom)") — the family-as-financial-co-signer pattern that recurs.

Key quotes

  • "The RV is your beachhead. It's the secure, low-cost position you establish on the new shore. It's not the capital city you intend to conquer, but it's the base from which you'll launch your campaign."
  • "There is a pivotal moment, manipulate reality to your will. There is nothing more."
  • "Minimum required work for maximum freedom. Making it is not a joke."

Open questions

  • Did the RV plan ever actually deploy? (Per 2025 10 03 im moving to austin: no — Yahir got a 1BR apartment via mom's help. The RV stays in Oregon.)
  • The Sterling motorcycle / "ryker" mention in Tab 7 — got sold for cash for the move?
  • Brazil / Mongolia / Colombia side-quest list — were any executed?
